summary: First full testing run for Mo's studio landing site. Backend: 8/8 pytest pass (root, contact create valid, optional company, invalid email 422, missing fields 422, empty message 422, list returns array no _id, list sorted desc). Frontend: page loads, all sections present, all data-testids match spec (services x4, pricing x3, work x4, testimonials x3), all 3 LinkedIn links point to correct URL, pricing CTA scrolls to #contact, valid form submission shows success toast and clears fields and persists to backend via GET /api/contact, no console errors. One minor mismatch: empty-form submission triggers native HTML5 validation popup (because Inputs use `required`) instead of the custom 'Please fill in all required fields' toast — toast only fires when Selects are missing.

integration_issues.issue: Spec expects a custom toast 'Please fill in all required fields' on empty submit, but the Input components have HTML5 `required` attribute which causes the browser's native validation tooltip to appear and short-circuits onSubmit, so the toast never fires for missing name/email/message. Toast does fire if only Selects (project_type/budget) are missing. Either remove `required` from Inputs or move validation before the native form-submit (e.g., novalidate on form, or validate onClick).

action_items: (Minor) Fix empty-form validation UX: add `noValidate` to <form> in App.js Contact, or remove `required` from Inputs, so the JS toast fires instead of the native browser tooltip. This will satisfy the explicit spec line 'empty submission shows error toast Please fill in all required fields'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `logger` is defined AFTER it is referenced inside POST /api/contact (logger is configured at module bottom, after include_router and middleware). This works because the route function body only executes at request time when logger is already bound to module, but it is fragile — move the logging.basicConfig + logger definition to the top of the file.

server.py: GET /api/contact sorts by `created_at` field, but `created_at` is stored as ISO string (line 93). String sort happens to be chronological for ISO-8601 with same timezone, so this currently works; document this constraint or store as native datetime.
App.js is 930 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into /components/{Header,Hero,Services,Pricing,Portfolio,Testimonials,Contact,Footer}.js for maintainability.
Contact form: `required` on Inputs conflicts with the JS-level toast validation (see frontend integration_issue above).
seed_data_creation: Pytest creates several TEST_-prefixed contact submissions in db.contact_submissions (not cleaned up — small footprint, prefixed for easy identification). Frontend e2e also created one TEST_e2e@example.com submission.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_mo_studio_api.py (pytest, 8 tests). Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. The only known issue is the contact form empty-submit toast not firing because of HTML5 `required` attributes — confirm this after main-agent fix by clicking submit on a fresh form (no fields filled) and asserting the sonner toast 'Please fill in all required fields' appears.
rca of the issue: Empty-submit toast missing: <Input> components in shadcn pass through the `required` HTML attribute. When the form is submitted with empty required Inputs, the browser's native constraint validation API blocks the submit event and shows a tooltip, so React's onSubmit handler (which would call toast.error) is never invoked. Fix options: (a) add `noValidate` prop to the <form> element so HTML5 validation is skipped and JS handler runs; or (b) drop `required` from the Inputs and rely solely on the JS check already present in onSubmit.
